blocks.text: Cynthia Hunter, Chief Operating Officer
{{fact:f:E-2024-001.client}}
Re: Trademark clearance engagement
Dear Ms. Hunter:

This letter confirms the terms on which Hollowell Patent Group PLLC will act for Jackson Inc in connection with the trademark clearance work we discussed. It is written to be read later, by people who were not in the room when we discussed it, and so it states the scope of the work, the conditions on that work, and the terms of payment before any of it begins. If a term below does not match your understanding of our conversation, tell me before you sign rather than after.
Scope and Approach

You have asked us to assess the availability of a set of proposed marks for use and for registration in connection with products Jackson Inc intends to bring to market. Our work will consist of knockout screening of the candidate marks, full clearance searching of the candidates that survive that screening, and written opinions on the marks you elect to pursue. Each opinion will address likelihood of confusion and registrability on the record available to us at the time it issues. It will not address rights outside the United States, common-law uses that the searches described below cannot reach, or the separate question of whether a particular third party will in fact object.

A clearance opinion is a judgment about risk on an incomplete record, and we will write it that way. Where the record supports a conclusion, we will state the conclusion. Where the record does not, we will say what we found, what we did not look for, and what remains open. We do not issue unqualified assurances that a mark is free to use, and I would encourage you to be skeptical of any firm that does.

items: Knockout screening of the candidate marks against federal registrations and pending applications, with a short written report identifying the candidates that do not survive and the references that stopped them.
Full clearance searching of the surviving candidates, covering federal, state, and common-law sources, including business name and domain usage.
Written clearance opinions on the marks you elect to pursue, each addressing likelihood of confusion, registrability, and the boundaries of the search on which it rests.
A closing conference to walk through the opinions and the filing options they leave open to you.

blocks.text: Filing and prosecution of any application is outside this engagement. If you decide to file, we will confirm that work in a separate letter with its own scope, its own fee, and its own dates. I raise it here because a deadline that attaches under an engagement nobody has written down is the kind of problem this firm exists to prevent.
The Team

I will supervise the matter and remain your point of contact. Ashley Williams, a patent agent in our prosecution group, will run the searching and draft the opinions. She has a standing objection to the word "clearly" appearing anywhere in an opinion letter, which I regard as a qualification rather than an eccentricity. Kelly Snyder, an associate, will handle the record review and will hold the docket for this matter. Every date on it is calendared the day it is known to us.

The matter will be staffed at the level each task requires and no higher. You will not be billed for two people reading the same document. If the composition of the team changes, I will tell you before it changes rather than in an invoice.
Conflicts

We have run this matter through our conflicts system and have identified no conflict that prevents us from acting for you. Our practice includes clients in medical devices, university licensing, and venture-backed hardware, and it is possible that a future matter will place this firm adverse to a company in which you have an interest. If that arises, I will raise it with you at once and we will resolve it under the applicable rules of professional conduct. This engagement is with Jackson Inc alone. It does not extend to affiliates, officers, or investors, and no other party may rely on the opinions we deliver under it.
Fee and Commencement

Our fee for the work described above is {{fact:f:E-2024-001.fee}}, fixed for the scope stated in this letter. The engagement begins on {{fact:f:E-2024-001.start}} and we expect the work to run approximately four months from that point, subject to the number of candidates that survive screening and to the responsiveness of sources we do not control. The fee covers the screening, the searching, the opinions, and the closing conference. It does not cover search vendor charges, official fees, or work outside the stated scope. Vendor charges are billed at cost. Work outside the scope is not performed until it is confirmed in writing.
Fees and Payment

Invoices issue monthly and are payable within thirty days of receipt. Amounts unpaid after sixty days accrue interest at the maximum rate the applicable law permits. If an invoice remains unpaid after ninety days, we may suspend work on the matter on written notice to you, and we may withdraw as described below. A suspension does not stop a deadline from running, and any notice of suspension will identify the dates then pending so that no one is left to discover them later.
Term and Termination

The engagement begins on the date stated above and ends when we have delivered the final opinion and held the closing conference, unless we extend it in writing. You may terminate at any time, with or without cause, on written notice to me. We may withdraw on written notice where the applicable rules of professional conduct permit, including for nonpayment, for a conflict arising during the representation, or where continuing would require us to act in a manner we consider improper. On termination you remain responsible for fees earned and costs incurred to that date, and we will return the file promptly on request. If any date is pending when the engagement ends, we will identify it in writing so that responsibility for it is unambiguous.
Confidentiality

Information you provide in the course of this representation is protected by the attorney-client privilege and by our duty of confidentiality, and we will treat the searches and opinions produced under this engagement as privileged work product. That protection is yours, which means it is also yours to waive, and it is easier to waive than most clients expect. Circulating an opinion beyond the people who need to act on it, or attaching one to a document produced in a dispute, can put it at issue. We will mark the opinions accordingly. Please limit their distribution, and ask us before an opinion goes to a party outside the company.
Limitation of Liability

Our work rests on the record available to us and on the information you give us. We do not warrant that a cleared mark will register, that no third party will assert a claim against it, or that any search has surfaced every conflicting use in existence. Our liability arising out of this engagement is limited to the fee paid under it, except to the extent the applicable rules of professional conduct do not permit that limitation, in which case it is limited to the fullest extent those rules allow. Nothing in this paragraph limits an obligation we may not lawfully limit.
Governing Law

This engagement is governed by the law of the jurisdiction in which the firm maintains its principal office, without regard to its conflict-of-laws rules, and by the rules of professional conduct applicable to our practice there. Any dispute arising out of this engagement will be resolved in the courts of that jurisdiction.

If the foregoing is acceptable, please sign below and return a copy to my attention. We will not open the file or order a search before the signed letter is back and the engagement date has arrived. If any term here raises a question, call me. I would rather answer it now than explain it to someone else later.
Very truly yours,

items: To: Kelly Snyder, Associate; Matthew Parrish, Patent Agent
From: Ashley Williams, Patent Agent
Re: Trademark clearance engagement for Jackson Inc, kickoff and workstreams

blocks.text: The engagement opens on {{fact:f:E-2024-001.start}} and this memorandum sets out how we will run it. The objective is a defensible clearance record for the candidate marks {{fact:f:E-2024-001.client}} has proposed, delivered in a form that will still make sense to a reader some years from now who was not party to any conversation we have about it. That reader is the audience for everything in this file. What we searched, what we did not search, and why we drew the boundary where we did belongs in the record at the time we do the work, not reconstructed from memory afterward.

We are at the front of the matter. Nothing in the file yet constitutes an opinion and nothing leaves this office described as one, in an email or otherwise. Cynthia Hunter is the client contact. Substantive correspondence goes to her, against the docket number, and into the file the same day it is sent.
Workstreams

items: Knockout screening. I will run the initial screen against federal registrations and pending applications and produce a short report identifying which candidates do not survive it. A candidate that fails the screen is documented with the reference that stopped it. We do not simply drop it off the list; a mark that disappears without a record is a mark somebody proposes again in six months.

Full clearance searching. Kelly Snyder will scope the vendor searches for the surviving candidates and reconcile the returns against the state and common-law sources. Organize the results by mark rather than by source. Each mark gets its own record and keeps it through the opinion.

Search boundaries and prior use. Matthew Parrish will define the limits of the search and draft the section stating what was and was not examined. That section is the part of an opinion most likely to be read by someone looking for a gap, and it will be written as though it already has been.

Opinions. I will draft. Kelly Snyder will review the record behind each conclusion before it goes out, and Matthew Parrish will confirm that no sentence in the analysis reaches past the boundary section into territory the search did not cover.
Docketing. Kelly Snyder holds the docket for this matter. Any date any of us learns goes to her the day we learn it, including dates that arrive by telephone from the client. Especially those.

blocks.text: Next Steps

items: Confirm the candidate list in writing with the client before any search is ordered. I am not willing to search a mark that was withdrawn in a meeting nobody memorialized, or to omit one that was added in the same meeting.

Confirm the intended description of goods in the same writing. Clearance is bound to the categories we search. An opinion written against one description does not travel to another, and the time to establish the description is now rather than at the draft.
Circulate the search parameters to the group for comment before the vendor order goes out. Comments in advance are cheap. A parameter we should have caught becomes a boundary problem in the opinion.
A short standing note each week: what moved, what is waiting, what is at risk. Two paragraphs is sufficient. Silence is not.

blocks.text: One matter of drafting practice, since we have a new file and it is easier to set the habit now than to correct it in the opinions. Write internal notes in the register you would use for a document that is filed. I do not maintain two registers, and I would rather not read one. A note written loosely because it was only for us has a way of becoming the exhibit that explains what we thought at the time.

The engagement runs roughly four months. That is enough time to do this properly and not enough to do it twice. Raise anything that looks like a scope or boundary question early. A question about scope is inexpensive this week and expensive in a draft opinion.
Working session on the trademark clearance matter for Jackson Inc. Minutes taken by Kelly Snyder.
Attendees

items: Ashley Williams, Patent Agent
Kelly Snyder, Associate
Cynthia Hunter, Chief Operating Officer, {{fact:f:E-2024-001.client}}

blocks.text: Discussion

Screening is complete. Of the candidates on the confirmed list, those that survived have gone to full search. Those that did not are in the file with the reference that stopped each one. Cynthia Hunter asked whether a screened-out candidate can be revisited. It can, but not quietly: it returns as a new candidate with its own record, and the earlier note stays where it is.

Full search returns are in for the surviving candidates and are being reconciled by mark. Ashley Williams flagged one candidate where the federal register is quiet and the common-law returns are not. Her position, which nobody in the room contested, is that the quiet register is the less interesting half of that record. The candidate will get a longer treatment in the opinion. It may not get a favorable one.

Scope of use took most of the session. Cynthia Hunter described product categories beyond the ones in the candidate brief we searched against. Ashley Williams noted that clearance is bound to the categories searched and that an opinion written against one description of goods does not carry over to another. The company will confirm the intended description in writing. If the description broadens, the search broadens with it, and that is a question of scope rather than of drafting. Ashley Williams asked that the confirmation come before drafting rather than during it.

Cynthia Hunter asked how the screening report should circulate inside the company. Ashley Williams answered that it is privileged work product, that the privilege belongs to the company rather than to us, and that it is easier to waive than most people assume. Distribution should be limited to the people who have to act on it. Anything going outside that group comes to us first. The same applies to the opinions when they issue, and more so, because an opinion attached to something produced in a dispute is an opinion put at issue.

Vendor returns. One set of common-law returns came back thinner than the parameters should have produced. Ashley Williams has asked for the search log rather than a re-run, on the theory that we ought to know what was looked at before we pay to look again. Pending the log, that mark is not being reconciled and no conclusion about it is in any draft.

Timing. We are at the midpoint of the engagement. Drafting will start on the candidates whose records are complete instead of waiting for the full set, so the opinions do not all land at the end. Two candidates are held pending the description of goods. No date on this matter changed at this session and the docket is current. I asked for the description-of-goods decision date at the top of the session and did not get one; the item is open on the list below without an owner date, which is the only thing on this matter currently in that condition.
Action Items

rows: Confirm the intended description of goods in writing
Drafting on two candidates is held until this arrives
Reconcile the remaining common-law returns and summarize the adverse uses on the flagged candidate
Longer treatment expected in the opinion
Circulate the search-boundary section for comment before it goes into a draft
States what was and was not examined
Open a separate record for any candidate revisited after screening
Earlier screening note stays in the file
Calendar any date arising from the description-of-goods decision

blocks.text: Next session to be scheduled once the description of goods is confirmed. Minutes circulated to attendees. Corrections to Kelly Snyder.
Status report on the trademark clearance engagement for Jackson Inc. Prepared by Ashley Williams, Patent Agent. Privileged and confidential; prepared for the client in anticipation of legal advice.
Status Summary

The engagement for {{fact:f:E-2024-001.client}} is at its midpoint and on schedule. Knockout screening is complete, full clearance searching has returned for every candidate that survived the screen, and drafting has begun on the candidates whose records are complete. No date on this matter has moved.

Two items require a decision from the company before the second half proceeds as planned. Neither is a surprise and both were discussed at the last working session. They appear under Risks below with what each one costs if it waits. Nothing in this report is an opinion on the availability of any mark. The opinions issue in writing, they issue over a signature, and they will say what record they rest on. A status report is not a substitute for one and should not be relied on as though it were.
Completed This Period

items: Candidate list confirmed in writing and a record opened for each mark on it.

Knockout screening completed against federal registrations and pending applications. Each screened-out candidate is documented with the reference that stopped it, so a decision to revisit one begins from a record rather than from someone's recollection of a call.
Full clearance searches ordered and returned for the surviving candidates, covering federal, state, and common-law sources, including business name and domain usage.
Search returns reconciled by mark. Kelly Snyder has the reconciliation current for every candidate but the last set of common-law returns, which are in hand and in progress.
Drafting begun on the candidates whose records are complete rather than held until the full set is in, which keeps the opinions from arriving in a single batch at the end.

blocks.text: Upcoming Work

items: Complete the reconciliation of the remaining common-law returns.

Draft the search-boundary section for each opinion: what was examined, what was not, and why the line falls where it does. If an opinion is ever read by someone looking for a gap, this is the section they read first, and it is drafted on that assumption.
Draft opinions on likelihood of confusion and registrability for the candidates the company elects to pursue.
Closing conference to walk through the opinions and the options they leave open.

blocks.text: Budget

The fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2024-001.fee}} covers the screening, the searching, the opinions, and the closing conference. At the midpoint the work performed is consistent with that fee, and within the scope stated in the engagement letter I do not anticipate coming back to you about it. Search vendor charges are billed at cost and are within the range we discussed at the outset. The first risk below could take work outside the stated scope. If it does, you will hear from me before that work is done rather than after, and it would come to you as a separate letter rather than as an adjustment to this one.
Risks

items: Description of goods. Clearance is bound to the categories searched. The product categories described at the last working session are broader than the candidate brief we searched against. Drafting on two candidates is held until the intended description is confirmed in writing. If the description broadens, the search broadens, and that is a scope question with a fee attached to it. If it does not broaden, this item closes on receipt of one email.

One candidate with an adverse common-law record. The federal register is quiet for this mark and the common-law returns are not. It will receive a longer treatment and the treatment may not be favorable. If it is the mark the business wants, the time to talk about it is before the opinion is drafted rather than after the analysis exists in writing.

Sources outside our control. Part of the common-law investigation depends on records held by third parties and returned on their schedule. It has not cost us a date and I do not expect it to. It is the item on this matter with the least slack, which is why it appears here rather than in the section above.

Filing is not within this engagement. If you decide to file on a cleared mark, that work requires its own letter and its own dates before any deadline attaches to it. Raised now it costs nothing. Raised late it costs a date, and a date is the one thing on a matter like this that cannot be recovered.

blocks.text: The docket for the matter is current and Kelly Snyder holds it. If a date changes on your side, or if one arrives from anywhere else, send it to her the day you have it rather than waiting for the next report. Questions on any item above can come to me directly.
